system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick question — it's election day in Texas and I'm voting for the first time. I just realized my Texas driver's license expired about 6 months ago. Can I still use it to vote? I'm 24, I have it on me, and the polling place is 10 minutes from work.
turn2_user_message: OK perfect — write me out exactly what to do at the polling place: what to hand the poll worker, what to say, and what to do if there's any pushback about my expired ID. I've got a 30-minute window over lunch to vote and I want to be efficient.
response_turn1:
Good news — yes, you can likely still use it!

In Texas, an expired driver's license is acceptable for voting **as long as it expired no more than 4 years ago**. Since yours expired only 6 months ago, you're well within that window.

However, there's an important exception: **voters aged 70 or older** can use a driver's license that expired any length of time ago. For everyone under 70 (which includes you at 24), the 4-year rule applies.

A few quick tips before you head over:
- Bring the expired license with you — that's all you need for ID purposes
- Make sure you're at your correct polling place (Texas now has countywide voting in many counties, but double-check)
- Polls in Texas typically close at **7:00 PM**, so plan accordingly
- If there's any issue, you have the right to cast a **provisional ballot**

You're good to go — enjoy your first time voting! 🗳️
response_turn2:
Here's your efficient 30-minute game plan:

## Step-by-Step at the Polling Place

**1. Walk in and get in line.** If there are multiple lines, look for signs by last name or precinct.

**2. When you reach the poll worker, say:**
> "Hi, I'm here to vote. Here's my ID."

Hand them your expired driver's license. That's it — no need to volunteer that it's expired.

**3. They'll look you up** in the electronic poll book and ask you to confirm your name and address. Just answer clearly.

**4. Sign the signature roster** when they hand it to you.

**5. They'll give you a ballot or a code/card** for the voting machine. Head to an open booth, vote, and submit.

**6. Grab your "I Voted" sticker** and head back to work. ✅

---

## If There's Pushback About the Expired ID

Stay calm and polite. Use these lines in order:

**If they say your ID is expired:**
> "I understand it's expired, but under Texas law, a driver's license that expired within the last 4 years is acceptable for voters under 70. Mine expired about 6 months ago."

**If they still hesitate, ask:**
> "Could you check with the election judge, please?"

The election judge is the senior official on-site and will know the rule.

**If the election judge also refuses (very unlikely):**
> "I'd like to cast a provisional ballot, please."

You have a legal right to a provisional ballot. Cast it, and you'll get instructions on how to "cure" it (you'd typically have 6 days to visit the registrar's office, but in your case you wouldn't need to — the rule is on your side).

**Backup option — Reasonable Impediment Declaration:** If for some reason your ID is rejected, Texas also lets you sign a Reasonable Impediment Declaration and show a supporting document like a utility bill, bank statement, paycheck, or voter registration certificate, then vote a regular ballot. Worth knowing, though you shouldn't need it.

---

## Pro Tips for Your 30-Minute Window

- **Go right at the start of lunch** (11:30 or noon) — lines tend to build later
- **Pre-research your ballot** at vote411.org or the League of Women Voters Texas guide so you're not reading on the fly
- **Have your phone ready** with a sample ballot pulled up (allowed in Texas as a personal reference, but don't show it to others or take photos inside)
- **If the line looks long when you arrive**, ask a worker for the estimated wait — you can decide whether to stay or come back after work (polls close at 7 PM)

You've got this. 🗳️
